_title: Profound Autism Accessibility Platform — Market Assessment Jan 2026
_summary: Market Assessment · January 2026 This analysis maps 50 organizations across technology, healthcare, education, and research sectors to identify potential partners, funders, and users for a profound autism accessibility platform. The platform includes longitudinal behavioral data, a voice-search interface, touch-to-hear content transformation, and service navigation tools for the estimated 500,000+

description: Launching "Career and Life Readiness" modules for 50M students. Seeking structured content to handle the Transition Cliff (Special Ed → Adult Services). The platform serves as a destination for IEP Transition Plans after students age out of the school system. Licensing Revenue Active (2025-2026 school year) Tech / Philanthropy
activity: Rolling out Work-Based Learning modules. Building API integrations for post-secondary transition data. Active RFPs for content addressing students with IEPs aging out of K-12 systems.

description: Only major AI company actively collecting disability data. Cognitive accessibility explicitly in scope. Be My Eyes partnership sets precedent for dataset collaboration. 2026 Open Call emphasizes Community Connection and Mental Health (which covers neurodiversity in their taxonomy). [ 🔗 Grant Program microsoft.com/.../ai-for-accessibility ](https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/ai/ai-for-accessibility) $25K + Azure credits Rolling; Summit March 2026 HealthTech / Social Services
activity: Running AI for Good Open Call with focus areas including caregiver support and neurodiversity. Partnering with Be My Eyes on disability data collection. Azure nonprofit credits program active.

description: Acquired Uno Health to move from searching for benefits to automating enrollment. Facing 2027 Readiness Challenge for Medicaid work requirements automation. Currently lacks deep Profound Autism filters (e.g., "accepts aggressive behaviors," "1:1 staffing"). Strategic Partnership / Acquisition Urgent (2027 Medicaid deadline) Research Foundation
activity: Integrating Uno Health enrollment automation. Building Medicaid redetermination workflows ahead of 2027 work requirements. Expanding filter taxonomy for complex disability populations.

description: Largest private autism research funder. Operates SPARK dataset. Explicitly seeking profound autism data historically excluded due to compliance barriers (participants cannot sit for MRIs or standardized tests). Home-based longitudinal data solves the "Missing Cohort" problem. [ 🔗 Funding Opps sfari.org/funding-opportunities/ ](https://www.sfari.org/funding-opportunities/) $50K-$500K (Pilot); $1M+ (Research) Annual cycle; needs university PI HealthTech / Social Services
activity: Expanding SPARK registry with focus on underrepresented phenotypes. Funding home-based data collection methodologies. Annual Pilot Award cycle open to novel data capture approaches.

description: Builds state infrastructure connecting healthcare to social services (NCCARE360 in North Carolina). Desperate for outcome data proving service stability. Competes with Findhelp for state Medicaid contracts. B2B Partnership; State Contract Active state RFP cycles HealthTech / ABA
activity: Expanding closed-loop referral networks in multiple states. Bidding on Medicaid managed care contracts requiring outcome tracking. Building specialized service taxonomies for complex populations.

description: Dominant ABA practice management platform. ISP reporting alignment and behavioral data standardization. Direct fit with observational data framework. Needs longitudinal outcome data to prove treatment efficacy to payers. B2B Partnership; Data Integration Ongoing; payer pressure increasing Federal Agency
activity: Building payer-facing outcome dashboards. Integrating home-based data collection into clinical workflows. Responding to insurance demands for treatment efficacy documentation.

description: Federal agency for independent living and rehabilitation research. Explicit mandate for community participation. Engineering solutions for cognitive disabilities in scope. Less competitive than NIH with more applied/practical focus. [ 🔗 ACL Research acl.gov/programs/research... ](https://acl.gov/programs/research-and-development) $100K-$1M Annual cycle; needs fiscal sponsor HealthTech / Payer Infrastructure
activity: Funding assistive technology development through Field Initiated Projects. Prioritizing community living and participation outcomes. Accepting applications for cognitive accessibility engineering.

description: Backend software for many health plans. Just launched FHIR integration with Findhelp. Needs audit-ready data proving patients are receiving care. The platform provides closed-loop verification. B2B Partnership Active (FHIR rollout ongoing) Government Contractor
activity: Rolling out FHIR-based social care integrations. Building closed-loop verification for HEDIS reporting. Connecting payer systems to community service networks.

description: Federal contractor for Medicaid eligibility systems across multiple states. Larger deployment footprint than Findhelp. Would consume eligibility determination logic at scale for redetermination workflows. [ 🔗 Gov Solutions maximus.com/solutions ](https://maximus.com/solutions) B2B Subcontract; Licensing Aligned with state contract cycles EdTech
activity: Operating Medicaid eligibility systems in 20+ states. Building redetermination automation for 2027 work requirements. Subcontracting specialized disability logic modules.

description: Dominant software for IEP/504 tracking in many states. Heavy focus on Medicaid claiming for schools. The platform maps services and funding eligibility (Medicaid waivers) aligning with their financial backend. Vendor Integration; Licensing Active (school year cycles) Research Foundation
activity: Building post-graduation outcome tracking for IDEA compliance. Integrating Medicaid waiver eligibility into IEP workflows. Expanding transition planning modules.

description: Primary champion of the "profound autism" label (Lancet Commission). Needs projects validating profound autism as a distinct category requiring distinct services. Platform demonstrates infrastructure gap. [ 🔗 Grants & Awards autismsciencefoundation.org... ](https://autismsciencefoundation.org/grants-awards-fellowships/) $25K-$100K Annual cycle; may need university Workforce Training
activity: Funding research validating profound autism as distinct clinical category. Supporting lifespan research addressing adult services gap. Annual training award cycle open.

description: Massive leader in training for social workers and Direct Support Professionals (DSPs). Need content showing what profound autism actually looks like. DSP workforce crisis means high demand for practical training materials. Content Licensing Deal Ongoing demand Tech / Philanthropy
activity: Expanding DSP certification content amid workforce shortage. Licensing video case studies for complex disability populations. Building competency-based training for high-support-needs clients.

description: Project Euphonia (atypical speech recognition) is the primary overlap. Project Relate provides communication support for non-standard speech. Google.org Impact Challenge offers funding for nonprofits using AI for economic inclusion. [ 🔗 Project Euphonia sites.research.google/euphonia/ ](https://sites.research.google/euphonia/about/) $500K-$3M (Impact Challenge) Periodic; requires 501(c)(3) AAC Company
activity: Collecting non-standard speech samples for Euphonia model training. Deploying Project Relate for atypical speech users. Periodic Impact Challenge grants for AI accessibility projects (501(c)(3) required).

description: Core AAC provider for profound autism population. Eye-tracking technology for non-verbal users. Sell hardware but lack adult content—users graduate high school with nothing engaging on their devices. Content Licensing; Distribution Ongoing Housing / Services Nonprofit
activity: Expanding Boardmaker content library. Seeking adult-appropriate educational content for post-secondary users. Building third-party content partnerships for device ecosystem.

description: Autism housing provider actively piloting technology. Immediate deployment opportunity for kiosk architecture. Real-world validation environment for platform in residential setting. [ 🔗 Main Site firstplaceglobal.org ](https://firstplaceglobal.org) Pilot Site Partnership Active pilot opportunities
activity: Expanding First Place Global model to additional sites. Piloting assistive technology in residential units. Evaluating daily living support systems for residents.
